researchLabGuide.title: How to Use Dr. Claw
researchLabGuide.description: Use the inno-pipeline-planner skill in Chat to start a 5-stage research pipeline, then review tasks and artifacts here in Research Lab.
researchLabGuide.step1: Go to Chat and ask the agent to use the inno-pipeline-planner skill for your research topic, goal, or paper idea.
researchLabGuide.step2: Answer the clarifying questions so the agent can define your research brief and pipeline correctly.
researchLabGuide.step3: The agent creates your research brief and stage-based task pipeline.
researchLabGuide.step4: Return to Research Lab to review progress across the 5 stages: Survey, Ideation, Experiment, Publication, and Promotion.
researchLabGuide.step5: Click Go to Chat on any task to execute it, then refresh this page to inspect the latest artifacts and keep the pipeline moving.
researchLabGuide.interfaceMap: Interface: Task pipeline on the left, Artifacts Explorer and preview on the right. Click an artifact to inspect it or Go to Chat on any task to execute it.
researchLabGuide.readmeHint: For full process details, see README.md -> Usage Guide.
researchLabGuide.briefEditingStep1: Fill core constraints first (goal, boundaries, metrics). Incomplete inputs are acceptable for initial task generation.
researchLabGuide.briefEditingStep2: Click Apply Template + Generate Tasks to update the research brief and regenerate the task list.
researchLabGuide.briefEditingStep3: Use Use in Chat from the Pipeline Task List below in sequence, and keep writing clarified conclusions back to the research brief.

researchLabTaskBoard.editHint: Double-click task card or use Edit button

messages.networkError: Network error. Please check your connection.

surveyPage.title: Survey Stage
surveyPage.description: Review project-related papers, literature graphs, survey notes, and stage tasks in one place.

mainContent.selectProjectDescription: Select a project from the sidebar to start coding with Claude. Each project contains your chat sessions and file history.
mainContent.createProjectMobile: Tap the menu button above to access projects
mainContent.createProjectDesktop: Create a new project by clicking the folder icon in the sidebar

projectDashboard.title: Project Dashboard
projectDashboard.trashSubtitle: Deleted projects are moved here first so you can restore them or remove records and files separately.
projectDashboard.trashEmptyDescription: Projects deleted from the dashboard are moved here first instead of being erased immediately.
projectDashboard.trashDeleteTitle: Choose how to delete this trashed project
projectDashboard.trashDeleteDescription: Logical delete removes the database record only. Physical delete also permanently removes files still stored in trash.
projectDashboard.subtitle: Track every workspace and jump back into active work quickly.
projectDashboard.skillsDescription: All projects use the same installed skills. Manage and inspect them here from the global dashboard instead of inside each project.
projectDashboard.projectsSectionSubtitle: Open active workspaces, review progress, and jump into the next task without digging through the sidebar.
projectDashboard.emptyDescription: Create or add a workspace from the sidebar to start tracking project progress here.

fileTree.askAboutDirectoryPrompt: Please explain the folder `{{path}}`. What is its purpose, what are the important files or submodules inside it, and how does it fit into this project?

step2.githubHelp: Optional: provide a GitHub URL to clone a repository
step2.githubAuthHelp: Only required for private repositories. Public repos can be cloned without authentication.

step2.publicRepoInfo: Public repositories don't require authentication. You can skip providing a token if cloning a public repo.
step2.noTokensHelp: No stored tokens available. You can add tokens in Settings → API Keys for easier reuse.
